description: Python governance envelope for `.intent/workers/*.yaml` declarations. Source of truth for the YAML shape is `.intent/META/worker.schema.json`; this contract governs the Python dataclass that mirrors it (src/shared/workers/base.py, alongside the Worker base class that consumes it). IntentRepository.load_worker() returns the parsed YAML as a dict consumed by Worker.__init__ as self._declaration. Nested object fields (metadata, identity, mandate, implementation, config) are kept as dicts in the Python class because they cross the YAML→dict parse boundary as dicts — adding a layer of nested dataclasses would buy no enforcement power that META/worker.schema.json doesn't already provide for the YAML side. Closes the WorkerDeclaration Wave 1 deferral (JSONB-shaped gap, alongside ProposalConsequence and ConstitutionalViolationPayload, now all closed). Authority: ADR-056 Wave 1.

kind.description: Worker kind. Constitutional values: worker, shop_manager. Mirrors the kind enum in META/worker.schema.json.

metadata.description: Declaration metadata: id, title, version, authority, status, optional rationale. Shape governed by META/worker.schema.json.

identity.description: Worker identity: uuid (permanent constitutional UUID) and class (sensing/acting/governance/supervision). Shape governed by META/worker.schema.json.

mandate.description: Operational mandate: responsibility, phase, optional permitted_tools, scope, approval_required, schedule. Shape governed by META/worker.schema.json.

implementation.description: Python implementation pointer: module, class, optional requires_core_context, params. Shape governed by META/worker.schema.json.

config.description: Worker-specific runtime configuration read by the worker via self._declaration.get('config', {}). Optional. Values are not passed to the constructor.
